content.problemStatement:
In 197119711971, Chef and his wife created a beautiful array AAA  of NNN integers: A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã. The absolute difference between any two adjacent elements of the array was at most DDD. When he read about **Prefix Max** and **Prefix Min** in 200020002000, Chef made two more arrays BBB and CCC of NNN integers where, for iii ranging from 111 to NNN,


Bi=max(A1,.....Ai)B_i = max(A_1,.....A_i)Bi‚Äã=max(A1‚Äã,.....Ai‚Äã)
Ci=min(A1,.....Ai)C_i = min(A_1,.....A_i)Ci‚Äã=min(A1‚Äã,.....Ai‚Äã)
Now it is 2023. Unfortunately, Chef has lost the original array AAA. But fortunately he still remembers the arrays BBB, CCC, and the value of DDD. He is missing his late wife and array AAA a lot nowadays. He knows you are a great programmer and so asks for your help. He has given you the arrays BBB, CCC, and the value of DDD. Can you generate his lost array AAA?

If there are multiple arrays AAA which satisfy the constraints, you can print any of them. It is also possible that it is impossible to generate an array AAA using the information Chef remembered. If so, print "No".


Input Format

The first input line will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of 333 lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and DDD ‚Äî the number of elements in the array and the upper bound on the difference between any two adjacent elements of the array.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1‚Äã,B2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,BN‚Äã.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers C1,C2,‚Ä¶,CNC_1, C_2, \ldots, C_NC1‚Äã,C2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,CN‚Äã.




Output Format
For each test case,




If it is possible to generate the array AAA, print 2 lines-



In the first line print **"YES"** without quotes.
In the second line print NNN space-separated integers, the array A1A_1A1‚Äã...ANA_NAN‚Äã.



Otherwise, print **"NO"** in one line without quotes.




You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings YES, yEs, yes, and yeS will all be treated as identical).
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 5‚àó1055*10^55‚àó105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Testcase 1: Consider A=[1,2,3]A=[1, 2, 3]A=[1,2,3]. All adjacent differences are at most D=1D = 1D=1. And, the prefix max and prefix min arrays match the given BBB and CCC. So this is a valid answer.
